hook:
·˚✿ POLLINATIONS WEEKLY ✿˚·
vol.41 — new models, sdk upgrades & normalized schemas

41 PRs merged this week. We added new models, upgraded the SDK, and finally stopped letting your 4xx client errors tank our internal success rate monitors.
body:
───

→ THE MODEL ROSTER EXPANDS
We wired up suno-v5, step-3.5-flash, and flux-2-dev via api.airforce. We also added image input support for flux-2-dev and put in a fix to snap its dimensions to supported sizes. Turns out sending arbitrary pixel dimensions to an API that expects very specific boxes ends exactly how you'd expect.

→ SDK & INFRASTRUCTURE
The NPM package (@pollinations_ai/sdk) just got a major update with STT (Speech-to-Text), Media Upload, and Account Endpoints.

On the backend, we normalized the finish_reason output to match the OpenAI format because everyone expects that schema now. We also added abuse detection crons, started banning deleted GitHub accounts, and tweaked the media service to add Content-Disposition headers.

→ THE COMMUNITY APP AVALANCHE
Over 15 new apps hit the showcase this week, including Pollinations CLI, PromptLab Studio, and an AI Image Gallery. To handle the volume, we added a step to approve apps before PR creation so the repo doesn't turn into the wild west.
closing:
Everything is live and open source. Check the repo or build something weird.
github.com/pollinations/pollinations
image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a retro RPG quest board. A cute pixel art bee mascot (yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, two black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks on cheeks, a small happy smile, small rounded light blue translucent wings) wearing a tiny adventurer's hat is reading the board. The board features chunky retro UI panels with pixel text: 'WEEKLY WRAP', 'FLUX-2-DEV', 'SDK UPGRADES', '41 PRs'. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic, information-dense but beautiful, emotionally warm.
image_text: WEEKLY WRAP, FLUX-2-DEV, SDK UPGRADES, 41 PRs
reasoning: A dispatch format perfectly suits a 41-PR week. The tone is dry and observational, particularly pointing out the humor in fixing dimension snapping and 4xx errors skewing uptime metrics. Formatting uses plain text unicode dividers to create the requested zine/bulletin feel.

·˚✿ POLLINATIONS WEEKLY ✿˚·
vol.41 — new models, sdk upgrades & normalized schemas

41 PRs merged this week. We added new models, upgraded the SDK, and finally stopped letting your 4xx client errors tank our internal success rate monitors.

───

→ THE MODEL ROSTER EXPANDS
We wired up suno-v5, step-3.5-flash, and flux-2-dev via api.airforce. We also added image input support for flux-2-dev and put in a fix to snap its dimensions to supported sizes. Turns out sending arbitrary pixel dimensions to an API that expects very specific boxes ends exactly how you'd expect.

→ SDK & INFRASTRUCTURE
The NPM package (@pollinations_ai/sdk) just got a major update with STT (Speech-to-Text), Media Upload, and Account Endpoints.

On the backend, we normalized the finish_reason output to match the OpenAI format because everyone expects that schema now. We also added abuse detection crons, started banning deleted GitHub accounts, and tweaked the media service to add Content-Disposition headers.

→ THE COMMUNITY APP AVALANCHE
Over 15 new apps hit the showcase this week, including Pollinations CLI, PromptLab Studio, and an AI Image Gallery. To handle the volume, we added a step to approve apps before PR creation so the repo doesn't turn into the wild west.
